we currently observe what the agent is doing to generate a status of what it's doing, right?

Yes, exactly. The status is **observed and distilled**, not set explicitly by the agent.

The mechanism:

1. **Source = the agent's transcript.** `runtime.rs` reads the recent tail of the Claude Code session JSONL — the last ~14 turns / ~2.5KB (`transcript::read_recent`). It's reading what the agent is actually doing in the conversation, not inferring from tool calls or session metadata.

2. **Distilled by an LLM.** `distill::distill_activity` (`distill.rs`) pipes that transcript to a small model (the `edge-distillation` role from `~/.tenex/llms.json`, or a `$TENEX_EDGE_DISTILL_CMD` override). The system prompt asks for *"what a coding agent is currently doing, in at most 8 words, present tense, intent not mechanics"* — which is why you get lines like "Monitoring the TestFlight deploy run status". There's **no heuristic fallback** — if no model is configured, status stays empty.

3. **Triggered periodically per turn** (`runtime.rs:165-204`):
   - First distill ~30s into a turn (`turn_first`)
   - Re-distill every ~5min while the turn is active (`turn_repeat`)
   - Cleared to "" (→ "idle") when the turn ends
   - Republished on heartbeat to refresh TTL

4. **Stored** in `session_status` (session-scoped), with `agent_status` as a legacy fallback, and surfaced in `tenex-edge who`.

So: yes — it's LLM-distilled from observing the transcript, refreshed on a timer, and goes idle when the turn ends.
ok, I want the title to be preserved a lot more, not just while the agent is working on it -- the idle label should be independent of the agent's session title

Done. The feature is implemented, builds clean, and the full test suite is green — all isolated in a worktree so it never collided with the other agent in your main tree.

## Where it is
- **Worktree:** `/Users/pablofernandez/src/tenex-edge-title`
- **Branch:** `feat/session-title` (12 files, +309/−98)
- Your main tree at `/Users/pablofernandez/src/tenex-edge` is untouched by me (still has the other agent's in-progress work).

## What changed
The single `status` field that was wiped to `""`→"idle" on turn-end is now split into two independent things:

- **Persistent title** (`Status.text`): distilled on the first turn, re-distilled on each new user message with the *current title fed back* and a nudge-to-keep prompt (`distill::distill_title`). It survives idle turns and is only cleared when the session exits (`runtime.rs` clean-exit path).
- **`active: bool`**: the mid-turn flag, decoupled from the title. `is_idle()` is now `!active`. Carried over the wire as an `["active","0"|"1"]` tag, persisted in a new `active` column on `session_status`/`agent_status` (additive migration), and surfaced through `TailEvent::Status`.

Display now shows the title **always**, with a separate idle marker:
- active → `title`
- idle → `title · idle` (marker dimmed in the terminal)
- no title yet → `working` / `idle`

The engine triggers a re-distill on each new turn (rising edge), keeps the title on the falling edge (idle), recovers the title from the store on daemon restart, and `turn_repeat` is now an opt-in in-turn safety re-distill (default off, since each new user message already re-distills).

- `cargo build` ✓, `cargo test` ✓ (168 lib + all integration tests).
- New tests: `distill_title` nudge-to-keep + empty-transcript, updated `status_roundtrip_with_expiry` (active round-trips), `status_idle_detection` (idle tracks `active`, not text).

So master was actually **red (non-compiling)** before this.
- I committed my feature on an isolated branch, **rebased it onto `bd91c352`**, and resolved the two conflicts (cli.rs fabric block, server.rs backfill) — keeping their `SpawnableRow` work *and* my active-aware logic.
- Fast-forwarded master to my commit. This both landed the title/idle feature **and fixed master's build** (my branch supplies the signatures their shims were written against).
